The boxer is known for his power.
He fought in a first match.
He landed one hundred twenty punches.
That was the count for match one.
The second match came next.
He increased his punch count.
The increase was twenty five percent.
We must calculate that number now.
One hundred twenty times point two five is thirty.
Thirty added to one hundred twenty is one hundred fifty.
So he landed one hundred fifty in match two.
The third match followed after.
He landed thirty more than match two.
One hundred fifty plus thirty is one hundred eighty.
That is the count for match three.
Now we must find the total.
We add match one and match two.
One hundred twenty plus one hundred fifty is two hundred seventy.
Then we add match three to that sum.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty is four hundred fifty.
The total is four hundred fifty punches.
This is a very high number.
It shows his aggressive style well.
Power punches are hard to land.
